## Propellant Chemistry and Burn-Rate Laws

Solid propellants are typically classified as **double-base** or **composite**. The burning behavior is described by empirical laws that relate the linear burn rate \( r \) to chamber pressure \( p_c \).

### Vieille’s Law (Saint-Robert’s Law)

The most common model is:

$$
r = a \, p_c^n
$$

where:
- \( r \) = linear burn rate (m/s)
- \( a \) = burn rate coefficient (units depend on pressure units)
- \( n \) = burn rate pressure exponent (dimensionless, typically 0.2–0.7)
- \( p_c \) = chamber pressure (Pa or psi)

```{python}
#| label: burn-rate-law
#| echo: true
#| fig-cap: "Burn rate vs. chamber pressure for different values of n (a = 0.005 m/s·Pa⁻ⁿ)"

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def burn_rate(p, a=0.005, n=0.4):
    """Vieille’s Law - burn rate in m/s (SI units)"""
    return a * p**n

p = np.linspace(1e6, 10e6, 200)  # 1 to 10 MPa

plt.plot(p/1e6, burn_rate(p, n=0.3)*1000, label='n = 0.3')
plt.plot(p/1e6, burn_rate(p, n=0.4)*1000, label='n = 0.4 (typical)')
plt.plot(p/1e6, burn_rate(p, n=0.6)*1000, label='n = 0.6')
plt.xlabel('Chamber Pressure (MPa)')
plt.ylabel('Burn Rate (mm/s)')
plt.title('Effect of Pressure Exponent on Burn Rate')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()
